channel.title: World
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Netanyahu to push Trump to take tough Iran stance during White House visit – US politics live
item.description: Israeli PM is expected to press the president to take a harder line over Iran’s nuclear program Transportation secretary Sean Duffy said the El Paso a...

item.title: Putin ‘trying to break the Ukrainian people – they will not be broken,’ says Nato’s Rutte - Europe live
item.description: Head of alliance warns that Ukraine ‘cannot sustain this fight or secure this peace alone’ As part of the increased focus on Arctic and High North, UK...

item.title: Support for Trump’s immigration efforts drops sharply after Minnesota shootings
item.description: Nearly half (49%) of all American adults disapprove of handling of immigration, NBC poll finds, versus 34% in April Support for Donald Trump’s immigra...

item.title: No 10 ducks questions about whether Doyle controversy could result in his peerage being removed – UK politics live
item.description: Matthew Doyle, who stepped down as No 10 head of communications last March, had the whip removed on Monday Families of nurses and carers have said the...

item.title: Iran’s president denies it seeks nuclear weapon and admits ‘shame’ after mass protests
item.description: Masoud Pezeshkian tries to convey message of national unity as negotiations with US hang in balance US politics live – latest updates Iran’s president...

item.title: Serial rail fare evader fined £3,600 over 112 unpaid tickets
item.description: Charles Brohiri travelled without buying a ticket on 112 train journeys over a period of nearly two years.

item.title: Dancer Robin Windsor depressed after leaving Strictly, actress tells inquest
item.description: Actress Lisa Riley is among those giving evidence to the inquest of her late Strictly dance partner.

item.title: PM says peer did not give 'full account' over links to sex offender
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer was asked at Prime Minister's Questions about his decision to award a peerage to Lord Doyle.

item.title: Swiss bar owners face questions from victims' lawyers over fire disaster
item.description: Jacques Moretti is questioned by lawyers over the New Year's Day fire disaster in Crans-Montana that left 41 people dead.

item.title: What are Canada's gun ownership laws?
item.description: A deadly mass shooting in British Columbia is likely to increase scrutiny of whether Canada's gun laws should be toughened.
